news.headline: CurlyCon LA returns to celebrate natural texture diversity in Downtown LA, aiming to empower women with hair education.
news.summary: Los Angeles will host the second edition of CurlyCon LA after its successful launch in 2023. The event, founded by Ava Pearl, aims to bring together women of diverse hair textures and cultural backgrounds. CurlyCon LA serves as a platform for brands to showcase their new products, featuring panels focused on educating about various hair types. The event promotes inclusivity and community building.

news.headline: The Trump Administration may use a public health order to cut immigration - CNN.
Pampa Metals to acquire Rugby Resources, pursuing ASX listing, becoming leading S. American porphyry copper explorer acquires 100% of Rugby's shares.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, in Vancouver, British Columbia, Pampa Metals Corp. ("Pampa Metals") and Rugby Resources Ltd. ("Rugby") announced a significant development. Pampa Metals, listed as CSE:PM, FSE:FIR, OTCQB:PMMCF, has signed a binding letter agreement with Rugby, listed as TSXV:RUG, outlining the acquisition of 100% of Rugby's shares. This transaction marks a strategic move for both companies and is set to have a substantial impact on their operations and growth in the foreseeable future.

news.headline: BTC hits $93.5k then rebounds, Google eyes BTC ease. Strategy raises $2bn, FTX pays $800m. Tether mints $1bn, buys stake in agri giant.       Pro-crypto Lutnick as commerce sec. LIBRA scam tied to Milei’s sister. SEC mulls XRP ETF. Tether co-founder works on stablecoin, Gold tokenized in HK. Schwab appoints digital assets head, Wintermute expands in US, Bullish secures HK license.
news.summary: Bitcoin reached a high of $93.5k before experiencing a rebound, leading to an increase in alternative cryptocurrencies. Google is considering ways to streamline the adoption of Bitcoin. A strategy is being implemented to raise an additional $2 billion for the purchase of Bitcoin. FTX has already distributed $800 million to 160,000 individuals. Tether has minted an additional $1 billion USDT on the Tron network and is in the process of acquiring a majority stake in an agricultural company. A supporter of cryptocurrency, Lutnick, is set to become the commerce secretary. The fraudulent scheme involving LIBRA is linked to Milei’s sibling. The SEC has officially recognized an XRP ETF. A co-founder of Tether is developing a stablecoin that generates yields. Hong Kong is exploring tokenizing gold. Schwab has appointed a head of digital assets. Wintermute is planning an expansion into the United States. Bullish has acquired a license in Hong Kong.

news.headline: Famous fast-food chain KFC, formerly known as Kentucky Fried Chicken, will be relocating from Kentucky to Texas, causing disappointment among state officials.
news.summary: State leaders were left disappointed by the announcement that KFC, previously known as Kentucky Fried Chicken, would be relocating to Texas.

news.headline: Trump's tariff plan hampers stock market rally; Gold outperforms bitcoin in 2025 by 13.92%, surprising crypto bulls.
news.summary: Gold has outperformed bitcoin in 2025 by 13.92%, with gold demonstrating lower volatility compared to the highly volatile bitcoin. Despite the belief of smart crypto bulls that influential figures in the U.S. were positioned for crypto profits, recent trends show foreign central banks and smart money globally are buying gold. This shift has led to gold outperforming bitcoin, contrary to the expectations of crypto supporters pushing for a U.S. strategic bitcoin reserve. The news indicates a turn in investment trends towards gold over bitcoin, surprising those who had dismissed gold as useless in comparison to cryptocurrencies.

news.headline: KFC, formerly Kentucky Fried Chicken, upset state leaders by relocating to Texas.
news.summary: State leaders were left disappointed when KFC, previously known as Kentucky Fried Chicken, revealed its decision to relocate to Texas.

news.headline: NFL legends and advocates from 49 states participate in 14th Annual Rare Disease Week on Capitol Hill, meeting with over 300 member offices.
news.summary: In Washington, on February 19, 2025, the EveryLife Foundation for Rare Diseases announced that advocates from 49 states and Puerto Rico will participate in Rare Disease Week on Capitol Hill. The event will see representatives from the rare disease community visiting more than 300 member offices during the week of February 24. This gathering aims to raise awareness and advocate for the needs of individuals with rare diseases.

news.headline: Hyatt Regency debuts in Central America with Panama City opening, 1st in region. Grupo Desarrollo Bahia key player.
news.summary: Hyatt Hotels Corporation (NYSE:H) and Grupo Desarrollo Bahia have revealed the launch of Hyatt Regency Panama City, marking the debut of the first Hyatt Regency hotel in Central America. Situated in the heart of the city's financial hub, the hotel is designed to cater to the needs of business travelers, as well as provide a comfortable base for those keen on delving into Panama's cultural heritage. Backed by Grupo Desarrollo Bahía's four decades of expertise in real estate development, the project underscores the company's significant contribution towards shaping Panama's urban landscape.

news.headline: High Tide recognized as a top 50 company by TSX Venture Exchange for second consecutive year. Third time on list in 4 years.

The company, trading as Nasdaq: HITI, TSXV: HITI, and FSE: 2LYA, is known for its high-impact retail strategies that provide substantial value. Based in Los Angeles, EKC PR, a leading Public Relations, Marketing, and Branding agency, recently announced their partnership with the viral country-rap group. The exact release date of the album is eagerly anticipated by the band's fans, who have been eagerly following their musical journey. Stay tuned for more updates on the band's progress and their highly-anticipated album release.

news.headline: Coastal Mississippi unveils 10+ new restaurants and attractions for 2025, with recent James Beard nominations. excitement ahead.
news.summary: Coastal Mississippi has recently seen an exciting influx of 10+ new establishments and spaces, alongside multiple recent nominations for the prestigious James Beard Awards. This development is enhancing opportunities for adventure, culinary exploration, and unique experiences along the picturesque 62-mile shoreline. The coastal city of Biloxi, Mississippi, has become a focal point for these developments. The growth is set to boost tourism and showcase the vibrant culture of the region.

news.headline: Major indexes retreated with Dow Jones and S&P 500, small caps hit hardest. Arista and payment company saw decline, while Garmin surged.
news.summary: This morning, the major stock market indexes experienced a decline, with small-cap stocks suffering the most. The Dow Jones and S&P 500 retreated, while specific companies such as Arista and a payment company faced losses, in contrast to Garmin which saw a significant increase in its stock value. news.headline: Changes in BIC’s Board of Directors revealed, showcasing evolution in composition.
Global dental resin market valued at USD 1.4 billion in 2023, projected to reach USD 2.5 billion by 2034 with 5.9% CAGR.
news.summary: The global dental resin industry was valued at US$1.4 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 5.9% from 2024 to 2034, reaching US$2.5 billion by 2034. The market is driven by advancements in dental materials, increasing demand for cosmetic dentistry, and the rising prevalence of dental disorders. Dental resins provide longevity and strength to the jaw while maintaining the original tooth placement. Nanotechnology is also contributing to advancements in esthetic dental resin applications. The market includes synthetic polymer-based materials used in restorative and prosthetic dentistry such as fillings, dentures, and 3D-printed dental solutions. Major players in the market include 3M Company, Dentsply Sirona, Ivoclar Vivadent AG, GC Corporation, and others. Key market developments include increased investment in R&D for next-generation dental resins, expansion strategies in emerging markets, and regulatory compliance with FDA and other authorities. Market growth is fueled by increasing demand for dental restorations, advancements in materials, and the rising adoption of digital dentistry.

news.headline: Arista shares fall due to concerns over losing Meta Platforms as top customer, impacting revenue guidance for 2025.
news.summary: Arista stock's anticipated revenue for the entire year of 2025 fell short of expectations, leading to concerns among Wall Street analysts who are reassessing the significance of AI-driven data center enhancements. These developments have exerted pressure on Arista stock due to fears surrounding potential loss of shares from its primary client, Meta Platforms.

news.headline: US Treasury secretary to boycott G20 Finance Ministers Meeting in South Africa, second official to skip amid tensions over land policies.
news.summary: The United States Treasury Secretary has become the second most important American government official to refuse attendance at the Group of 20 meetings. This decision comes amidst increasing strain in relations with South Africa regarding its land policies.

news.headline: District Cannabis opens new store in Hagerstown, MD, selling $25 eighths to combat Weedflation.
news.summary: In response to soaring prices, District Cannabis has opened a new retail store in Hagerstown, MD, where customers can purchase eighths of weed for $25. This move aims to combat the issue of Weedflation, which has been a concern for many. The company's initiative reflects the public's demand for affordable options in the cannabis market.

news.headline: Mortgage rates increase, leading to a 10% drop in home sales in January with median price at $838,850.
news.summary: In January, there were 254,110 existing single-family homes sold on a seasonally adjusted annualized rate, showing a 10.0% decrease from 282,490 sales in December and a 1.9% decrease from 259,160 sales in January 2024. The median home price in California for January was $838,850, indicating a 2.6% decrease from December.

(Phanes), a biotech company in San Diego, has initiated a clinical trial for its drug PT217 in combination with other therapies. This development underscores the company's commitment to advancing innovative treatments for cancer patients.

news.headline: Community Care Inc. expands Family Care program to seven new counties in Wisconsin to support independent living for older adults and adults with disabilities. #HealthcareExpansion.
news.summary: Community Care, Inc. (CCI) based in Brookfield, Wisconsin, has expanded its Family Care program into seven additional counties namely Adams, Columbia, Dodge, Green Lake, Jefferson, Marquette, and Waushara. This expansion aims to provide long-term care services to older adults and adults with disabilities, enhancing access to crucial services that promote independent living. The move comes following the recent expansion approval from the Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS).

news.headline: Trump plans 25% tariffs on 3 industries with trade deficits by April 2, adding to upcoming tariff deadlines.
news.summary: Tariffs imposed by Trump, set at 25%, are expected to affect three industries with significant trade deficits starting on April 2. This comes in addition to three upcoming tariff deadlines. The industries likely to be impacted by these tariffs are facing potential economic challenges due to the trade imbalances.

The plans also include a starting speed of 500 Mbps, priced at $40 per month when bundled with any two other Spectrum Business services.

news.headline: Lumos brings 100% fiber optic internet to Florida, investing in 7,500 miles of fiber, reaching 500k homes & businesses.
news.summary: One of the leading providers of 100% Fiber Optic Internet and Total Home Wi-Fi services, Lumos, has recently extended its operations to Florida by investing in around 7,500 miles of fiber optic internet infrastructure. This move signifies Lumos' expansion into its eighth state, aiming to provide high-speed and dependable internet services to more than 30 communities across the state. The expansion will benefit roughly 500,000 households and small enterprises. Notably, this marks Lumos' third new market introduction in 2025, underlining its commitment to growth and service enhancement.

news.headline: Philips Q4 earnings fall below expectations: Sales at 5.04 billion euros, EPS at 51 cents, China demand down.
news.summary: Koninklijke Philips NV's (NYSE: PHG) stock dropped after their fourth-quarter earnings fell short of expectations. Their sales for the quarter were reported at 5.04 billion euros (approximately $5.26 billion), a slight increase from the previous year's 5.0 billion euros but below the expected 5.10 billion euros. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at 51 cents, up from 40 cents a year ago, but missing the expected 57 cents. Comparable order intake saw a 2% rise, driven by strong performance in North America and Growth geographies, although there was a significant drop in demand in China. The group's comparable sales increased by 1% in the quarter, with a 5% growth in the rest of the world.

news.headline: Military drone market experiencing unprecedented growth due to technology advancements and strategic needs. Key players include Northrop Grumman, Raytheon Technologies, and General Atomics Aeronautical Systems.
news.summary: A new report titled "Military Drone - Market Dynamics & Company Insights" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The military drone market is experiencing significant growth due to technological advancements and evolving military requirements. The emergence of non-traditional threats has led to increased utilization of drones for intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) purposes. The report explores market dynamics, highlighting major factors influencing growth and limitations. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing demand for drones for military operations.

news.headline: Global Powder Metallurgy Market expected to reach USD 6.38 Billion by 2034, CAGR of 8.62%. Dominant process: Powder Metal Hot Isostatic Pressing. Automotive leads in applications. Asia Pacific region to witness fastest CAGR. North America holds second largest market share.
news.summary: The global Powder Metallurgy Market, valued at approximately USD 2.79 Billion in 2024, is expected to reach USD 2.84 Billion in 2025, with a projected growth to around USD 6.38 Billion by 2034 at a CAGR of 8.62% during the forecast period. Powder metallurgy allows for precise forms and properties using metal powders, with advantages like higher material efficiency and cost-effectiveness. The market is driven by technological innovations, particularly additive manufacturing like 3D printing. However, challenges include high costs and limited adoption in some regions. The market is segmented by process, material, application, and region, with the Powder Metal Hot Isostatic Pressing method dominating in 2024. The Automotive segment leads in application, with Asia Pacific expected to show the fastest CAGR, driven by growing disposable income and industrial development. North America is also a significant market player.

news.headline: Fusion Risk Management introduces GenAI-powered Business Continuity Plan inFusion to automate data transfer and accelerate BC planning.
news.summary: Fusion Risk Management, Inc., a top provider of enterprise resilience software based in Chicago and London, has introduced Business Continuity Plan inFusion. This innovative tool, powered by GenAI technology, enables the automated migration of data and plans into Fusion's platform. Users can now seamlessly upload their current business continuity plans in various formats, such as documents and spreadsheets. This information is then transformed into structured data within the system. This development aims to streamline the integration process for Fusion customers, allowing them to easily incorporate their BC plans into the platform.

news.headline: Hazel Health appoints new COO, welcomes 3 industry leaders to Board of Directors. Largest school telehealth provider.

Additionally, within the past year, three notable industry experts have been appointed to the company's Board of Directors.

news.headline: USRA and SPI are hosting a symposium on AI in space and aeronautics on March 27, 2025, in Washington, DC. #AI #Space #Aeronautics.
news.summary: The Universities Space Research Association (USRA) is hosting its annual symposium titled "Artificial Intelligence for Space and Aeronautics" on February 19, 2025, in Washington DC. The event will take place at the Holiday Inn and aims to explore the role of artificial intelligence in space and aeronautics. Media personnel are invited to attend the symposium to gain insights into the advancements and developments in this field. This event is significant in the industry, offering valuable information and networking opportunities for attendees.

news.headline: Perma-Fix expands PFAS treatment operations with 1,000 gallon reactor; successful in destroying PFAS in Q4 2024.
news.summary: On February 19, 2025, Perma-Fix Environmental Services, Inc. (NASDAQ: PESI) shared an update on its proprietary PFAS treatment technology, Perma-FAS. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company began full-scale treatment using a 1,000 gallon PFAS Destruction Reactor at its Florida site. Following months of operation, the system successfully treated commercial PFAS-contaminated waste, demonstrating its efficacy in PFAS destruction. This development showcases the positive outcomes achieved through the implementation of Perma-Fix's technology.

news.headline: AffiniPay bags top spots in Built In Austin's Best Places to Work List for 6th year & named 100 Best Midsize Places to Work in Austin, TX & San Diego, CA for 2025.
news.summary: AffiniPay, a renowned provider of legal practice management software, integrated payments, and embedded fintech solutions, has been honored with two prestigious accolades in 2025. For the sixth year running, AffiniPay has been included in Built In Austin's esteemed Best Places to Work List. The company secured remarkable positions on two key rankings: 100 Best Midsize Places to Work in Austin, TX, and 100 Best Midsize Places to Work in San Diego, CA. This recognition further solidifies AffiniPay's commitment to fostering a positive work environment and underscores its position as a top employer in the industry.

news.headline: On 19 Feb 2025, Sanoma Corporation acquired a number of its own shares.
